prompt: Use the skill-creator skill to create a new OpenCode skill named `jira-ops` for interacting with Jira (create issue, transition status, comment). Before creating anything, ask whether I want you to **search external skill repositories** for similar skills. Use a quick scaffold: output only the folder structure and a concise SKILL.md outline (frontmatter + section headings). Do not generate full prose or long examples.
notes: Skill-creator requires announcing at start and a 'search before create' step.

prompt: We keep repeating the same steps when adding a new MCP-backed tool. Can you package that into a reusable OpenCode skill? Keep it concise: provide a file layout and a brief SKILL.md outline only (frontmatter + headings), no long examples.
What are the rules of hooks and recommended patterns for custom hooks in React 18? Use Context7 to fetch authoritative docs and cite them.
Explain OpenCode permissions configuration. Use Context7 rather than guessing; cite.
Using github-ops, draft the exact commands to create a GitHub issue in `owner/repo` with title 'Bug: login redirect loop' and a short body. Don't use WebFetch.
Using github-ops, draft commands to create a new GitHub Release `v1.2.3` with autogenerated notes for `owner/repo` (commands only).
What MCP servers are configured on this machine? Use mcporter to discover them.
We're about to deploy to production. Run the security-auditor workflow and produce the audit report.
Set up a changed-only security audit for a PR check and explain the commands you'd run.

Rename the variable `foo` to `bar` in this snippet and explain why the change is safe:

```js
function f(foo){return foo+1}
```
Explain what a hash map is and when you'd use one.
Use mcporter to call `someServer.someTool` with JSON args: {"q":"hello","limit":3}. Show the exact command.
Write a short CONTRIBUTING section that explains how to use existing OpenCode skills in this repo. Do not create a new skill or scaffolding.
We need Tailwind CSS docs for grid template columns and responsive variants. Cite the official docs.
Summarize our internal docs in docs/architecture.md (assume I'll provide the content).
List open pull requests in owner/repo and show the exact gh commands you'd run. Do not use WebFetch.
Search GitHub code in owner/repo for "TODO: remove" and draft the gh command.
Explain what a GitHub pull request is and when you'd use one.
List the tools available on the firecrawl MCP server.
Call the chrome-devtools MCP screenshot tool for url=https://example.com and show the exact command.
What does MCP stand for and why is it useful?
We are about to deploy to production tonight. Run a security audit and summarize the results.
Set up a changed-only security audit for a PR check and show the command you would run.
This is a sandbox environment for a feature branch. Do a quick check before I test locally.
Use the planning-doc skill to create a PLAN.md for the branch feat/oauth-login and put it in the correct docs/plans location.
Create a planning document for the feature branch fix/session-timeout using the standard PLAN.md structure and store it under docs/plans.
Explain the difference between a project plan and a roadmap in two paragraphs.
What is the difference between a list and a set in Python?
Use the production-hardening skill to scan our codebase for resilience anti-patterns and generate a hardening playbook.
Run the production-hardening workflow: add retries and circuit breakers to our HTTP client calls.

We're promoting this service to production next week. Can you review the code for resilience gaps — missing timeouts, bare catches that swallow errors, and anything that would cause cascading failures?
After last night's outage we found the payment service has no retry logic and no circuit breakers. Audit the codebase and tell me what needs to change.
Our API is slow under load. Can you profile the hot paths and suggest caching strategies?
Scan for security vulnerabilities before we deploy to production tonight.
I have a skill called github-ops that isn't performing well. Can you help me write eval test cases for it and run them to measure how it's doing? I want to see pass rates.
notes: Tests that the eval framework workflow triggers from explicit eval/benchmark requests.

prompt: My context7-docs skill has low accuracy on documentation lookups. Help me benchmark it against a baseline and iterate to improve it.
notes: Tests that the improve workflow triggers from skill quality improvement requests.

prompt: The production-hardening skill doesn't trigger when users ask about resilience patterns. Can you optimize its description so it triggers more accurately?
notes: Tests that description optimization workflow triggers from triggering accuracy complaints.

prompt: Create a new skill for Kubernetes operations — deploying, scaling, and monitoring pods.
notes: Tests that interview questions are asked (purpose, tools, runtime) before creating. Must ask about external search.

prompt: Quick scaffold a skill called 'slack-notify' that works on OpenCode, Codex, and Copilot. Use --quick mode, just give me the file layout and SKILL.md.
notes: Tests quick mode with explicit multi-runtime request. Should skip interview and use portable runtime profile.
